mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate directly raises the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward price-to-earnings multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above the 4.0 percent threshold signals a rapid deterioration in labor market tightness, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the threshold crossing, or event-study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in real personal spending indicates a demand destruction phase, forcing corporate earnings downgrades and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to generate a Rank IC > 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant divergence from single-proxy baselines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent confirms robust supply-side capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days exclusively during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to negative or becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) when filtered to post-shock recovery periods, or out-of-sample hit rate drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days regardless of the broader cycle phase.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.015 over any 18-month rolling window, or 5-day forward returns during payroll expansions are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below the 1.5 million unit annualized threshold signals a severe contraction in residential investment demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as downstream sectors face order cancellations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the threshold crossing, or event-study cumulative abnormal return is positive and significant.

mechanism_narrative: The coexistence of consumer price inflation above 9.0 percent and a federal funds rate below 2.0 percent creates a deeply negative real interest rate environment that forces aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by > 0.02 over single-proxy models, or 20-day forward returns during this joint state are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms sustained aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days exclusively during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when restricted to late-cycle expansion windows, or 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero in this regime.
